checks.finding: T-002 (Risk: medium) authors contracts/capability-summary.schema.json, a new externally-visible API contract (design.md Cross-Layer Dependencies: 'Every schema and script this feature defines -> consumed by Epic A5's Capability Resolver'). risk-classification-policy.md's `high` tier names 'public API contracts' as a sensitive surface requiring Risk: high, and explicitly states 'An agent MUST NOT self-certify its own risk tier as the basis for relaxing a gate.' T-002's own Risk Rationale relaxes the tier via self-certified reasoning ('no such Gate reads this artifact yet... not a silently-corrupted one a downstream Gate trusts'), which is exactly the self-certification the policy forbids as sufficient justification. Under the declared medium tier, T-002's Required Workflow is acceptance-first rather than tdd, so it does not carry HIGH-CRITICAL-EVIDENCE's mandatory Red->Green/independent-review Done When items for a brand-new schema contract Epic A5 will build against.

All three declared Risk: high tasks (T-001, T-003, T-004) carry a 'TDD evidence' Done When item requiring RED and GREEN logs at specs/epic-192-a4-facet-manifest/verification/T-00N/{red,green}-sh.log plus 'An independent quality-gate verdict (a named reviewer distinct from the implementing agent) records PASS.' No task is declared Risk: critical, so the second-approver/signed-evidence-bundle requirement does not apply to any task.

No task's Done When list exceeds 8 top-level verifiable items (T-001: 6, T-002: 5, T-003: 7, T-004: 5, T-005: 7). Each task's title/Scope names a single coherent contract-plus-validator-plus-tests unit consistent across T-001..T-005 (schema/script authoring + its own test suite + CI/vendoring wiring), matching this repo's own cited Epic A2 precedent; T-005 additionally carries an explicit 'Note on scope breadth (TASK-SIZE)' justifying its two-work-area bundling. No fragmentation: no three-or-more tasks describe only a single function/file edit.

Every functional task's Done When cites acceptance criteria with explicit error-path/edge-case fixtures, e.g. T-001 AC-003 (duplicate rejected/empty-array accepted), AC-004 (applied/reason if-then-else both branches), AC-009 (malformed digest rejected), AC-028/047/048 (semantic-diagnostic negative fixtures); T-002 AC-014/AC-029 (extra-property and missing-field rejection); T-003 AC-030/AC-042 (still-array-shaped rejection, oneOf both/neither rejected); T-004's entire REQ-004/REQ-005 branch table (AC-023, AC-024, AC-044, AC-046) is explicitly fail-closed/argument-error focused. No functional task's referenced ACs are happy-path-only.

Every test suite each task authors is scoped and labeled as acceptance-first/TDD suites mapping 1:1 to specific AC-NNN rows (e.g. T-001's Done When cites AC-001..AC-011, AC-017/018, AC-028, AC-041, AC-047/048 against tests/facet-manifest-schema.tests.sh and tests/facet-manifest-semantics.tests.sh; T-005's parity suite cites AC-031/032/033/043), satisfying the acceptance-test criterion of referencing a specific AC-NNN. No suite is mislabeled as unit/integration/e2e inconsistent with its actual scope.

All three Risk: high tasks carry an explicit `Rollback:` field: T-001 ('revert this task's two commits (B then A, or both) via git revert... the staged .github/workflows/test.yml human-copy candidate... a revert re-stages a corrected candidate'), T-003 ('revert this task's two commits; nothing protected is written directly'), T-004 ('revert this task's two commits; nothing protected is written directly; the comparator itself writes nothing').

tests/run-all.sh/.ps1, the human-copy .github/workflows/test.yml candidate, and MANIFEST.sha256 are shared, append-only files edited by all of T-001..T-005, but tasks.md's Global Constraints fix a serialized T-001->T-002->T-003->T-004->T-005 append order, and each task's own Blockers field (T-002: T-001; T-003: T-001,T-002; T-004: T-001,T-002,T-003; T-005: T-001..T-004) enforces exactly that ordering transitively across every pair -- the 'downstream task... provided the Blockers field enforces the ordering' exception applies. CHANGELOG.md edits are each task's own new, non-overlapping bullet under the shared Unreleased header. tests/fixtures/facet-manifest/ additions are disjoint files within a shared directory, not the same file.

T-004's Blockers (T-001, T-002, T-003) include T-002/T-003 despite T-004's own Depends On text stating 'T-002/T-003 are not functional dependencies -- the comparator consumes only Facet Manifest instances'; however this is a disclosed, artifact-based (not spurious) blocker: T-002/T-003 write to the same append-only tests/run-all.sh/.ps1 array and human-copy test.yml candidate T-004 must append after, matching the same shared-file-serialization pattern SCOPE-DISJOINT already treats as legitimate. T-005's Blockers on all four prior tasks are a genuine functional dependency (its parity suite invokes all four scripts' outputs). No task's scope depends on another's output without a declared Blockers entry.
SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope.

findings.finding: T-002 is classified Risk: medium while authoring contracts/capability-summary.schema.json, a new externally-visible API contract consumed by future Epic A5 (design.md Cross-Layer Dependencies). risk-classification-policy.md's high tier names 'public API contracts' as a sensitive surface and explicitly forbids an agent self-certifying its own tier down from that trigger ('An agent MUST NOT self-certify its own risk tier as the basis for relaxing a gate') -- T-002's own Risk Rationale does exactly this ('no such Gate reads this artifact yet'). Under medium, T-002's Required Workflow is acceptance-first, not tdd, so it lacks the Red->Green/independent-review Done When evidence HIGH-CRITICAL-EVIDENCE would otherwise mandate for a new public schema contract.
